content.problemStatement:
An airline operates XXX aircraft every day. Each aircraft can carry up to 100100100 passengers.

One day, NNN passengers would like to travel to the same destination. What is the minimum number of new planes that the airline must buy to carry all NNN passengers?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing two space-separated integers XXX and NNN — the number of aircraft the airline owns and the number of passengers travelling, respectively.


Output Format

For each test case, output the minimum number of planes the airline needs to purchase.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and N — the number of aircraft the airline currently owns and the number of passengers that need to travel.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line: the minimum number of new planes the airline must purchase.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Required planes = ceil(600/100)=6, owned=4 → need 2 more.\nTest case 2: Required = ceil(523/100)=6, owned=3 → need 3 more.\nTest case 3: Required = ceil(245/100)=3, owned=8 → no additional planes needed.

hidden.label: edge case – exact multiple of capacity

solution.approach: Each plane can carry exactly 100 passengers. The total number of planes required to transport N passengers is ceil(N / 100). This can be computed without floating point as (N + 99) / 100 using integer division. If the airline already owns X planes, the additional planes needed are max(0, required - X). The algorithm processes each test case independently in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X = int(next(it))
        N = int(next(it))
        required = (N + 99) // 100
        ans = required - X if required > X else 0
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
